title: Security
lede: Simple, practical steps to keep your iMash account safe — strong passwords, controlling who's signed in, and limiting what each team member can reach.

steps.title: Use a strong password and reset it safely
steps.content:
1. Use a long, unique password for iMash that you don't reuse anywhere else.
  2. If you ever forget it, use Forgot password on the sign-in screen to get a secure reset link by email.
  3. Change a team member's password at any time from Team (User Management) by editing their user.

Never share one login between multiple people — give each person their own team member account instead so you can track and control access.

images.caption: Account security starts with per-person team accounts and controlled access
Account security starts with per-person team accounts and controlled access

steps.title: One active session holds control
steps.content:
• iMash allows only one active session at a time per account. If you (or someone using your login) sign in somewhere new, the older window is covered by a Take control lock screen.
  • Click Take control in the window you want to use — the other one stays locked until it takes control back.
  • If you see this lock unexpectedly, someone else may be using your login. Change your password right away.

steps.title: Give each person only the access they need
steps.content:
• Assign every team member a Role that grants just the read/create/edit/delete rights their job requires.
  • Keep Billing, Credentials (API keys), and User Management turned off for members who don't need them — these control money and access.
  • Use Permission Groups so a member only sees the records that belong to their team, not the whole account.

Manage all of this on the Team page.

steps.title: Deactivate access the moment it's no longer needed
steps.content:
1. When someone leaves or changes roles, open Team and use Disable on their row to block sign-in instantly.
  2. Re-enable later if they return — no need to delete and recreate the account.
  3. Rotate API Keys if a key may have been exposed, and only enable Credentials access for people who manage integrations.

steps.title: Your data stays isolated
steps.content:
• iMash keeps each account's data separated at the database level, so your records are never visible to other customers.
  • Within your account, roles, groups, and per-page toggles decide exactly what each member can open.
  • When the owner uses Impersonate to help a member, it's a temporary, time-limited view — the member's own login is never exposed.

support.content:
